system_name: NeuroGolf 2026 Headless Agent Web GUI + Guarded Kaggle Submission Pipeline

competition.submission_contents: At most one ONNX file per task, named task001.onnx through task400.onnx.
competition.objective: Use the NeuroGolf web GUI and headless scripts as the control plane. Generate or ingest candidate ONNX artifacts, run only allowed preflight and changed-task checks, package measured improvements, submit through the guarded Kaggle wrapper, then use the public leaderboard score as tactical truth.
constraints_to_verify: Submission zip opens and contains only valid taskXXX.onnx names.
No banned/disallowed operations or metric-loophole-only submissions.
No private, leaked, or unauthorized source material.
No credentials in logs, manifests, Hugging Face artifacts, or git.

available_now: private local best zip ignored by git
planned_or_optional: Hugging Face private artifact bus scripts

allowed_local_actions: changed-task compile/run scoring through scripts/score_candidate.py when it is fast
forbidden_local_actions: submitting more than the current Kaggle daily limit
submitting a zip without a promoted score report

candidate_promotion_requires: no test or arc-gen regression against current best when available
candidate improvement is described in the message
package_requires: private base submission zip exists locally
patched ONNX compiles from graph JSON
output zip SHA256 is recorded in sidecar manifest
submit_requires: Kaggle auth check passes without printing secrets
at least one promoted report is attached
last submission age is >= 180 seconds or no prior submission exists
no other candidate is in flight

phase_plan.goal: Authenticate Kaggle, smoke-check web GUI and local private best artifacts, and prepare the first guarded submission lane.
exit_criteria: private base submission zip exists locally
no expensive local tests are run

phase_plan.goal: Make exactly one guarded submission from a promoted candidate or known safe baseline, then stop for score readback.
exit_criteria: public score or pending status recorded

phase_plan.goal: Work on measured task updates, using changed-task fast scoring only and packaging only improvements.
exit_criteria: candidate reports exist for attempted tasks
failures and bugs are appended to errors.md
promoted candidates are packaged into zip variants

phase_plan.goal: Submit up to the remaining allowed improved zips, one at a time, respecting the 180-second cooldown.
exit_criteria: every submitted zip has at least one promoted report

phase_plan.goal: Summarize score movement, preserve best candidate, and rank next fixes from errors.md.
exit_criteria: best submission identified by public leaderboard score

orchestrator.mission: Own clock, gates, one-at-a-time policy, and stop/go decisions.

candidate_builder.mission: Create graph JSON candidates and compiler updates.

packager.mission: Patch promoted candidates into a private-base submission zip.

submitter.mission: Submit only through scripts/kaggle_guarded_submit.py and record public score/status.
hard_blocks: less than 180 seconds since last submit

append_for: missing scripts or missing private artifacts

allow_when_all_true: all attached reports have decision.promote == true
now - last_submission_at >= 180 seconds OR last_submission_at is null
no other candidate awaiting leaderboard score

graph_node_palette.dedupe_policy: The UI/config normalizes duplicate names; Add and Constant appear once even if listed multiple times in a prompt.
graph_node_palette.compile_policy: Nodes may be used in visual/imported graphs. If compile_graph does not yet support an op, add it deliberately through COMPILER_TODOS.md and tests before relying on it in a candidate.
graph_node_palette.source: union of GUI base ops and op types observed in the private best-submission ONNX manifest

graph_node_policy.missing_needed_node_rule: If a candidate or paper-derived solution requires a node/op that is not in the GUI palette or compiler, add the node deliberately instead of abandoning the solution.
required_steps: add the op to the GUI palette/input slot metadata
add compiler support or a documented COMPILER_TODOS.md entry when full support is not possible immediately
add backend tests for compile_graph behavior
add or update e2e coverage when the node must be selectable in the GUI
run pytest, frontend build, and relevant smoke/e2e checks
record any partial support or known limitation in errors.md during a run
graph_node_policy.hard_rule: Do not silently substitute a weaker graph when the missing node is the core primitive needed for the task family.
